title: O-1B reaches the arts SIX end-to-end — no O-1A-eight leak on any of her surfaces
expected: An arts case is screened against the O-1B six (lead role in distinguished productions, national/international recognition, reviews & press, commercial/critical success, recognition from orgs & experts, high salary) and NEVER shown 'scholarly articles' / 'patent' / 'judging'.
got: packFor('O-1B') returns the 6-criterion arts pack (packs.ts:99-141) with no Scholarly/Judging/Original/Patent criterion. The packFor() O-1A fallback can only fire for an UNKNOWN classification, but O-1B is a live program (jurisdictions.ts:63), so parseQualifyRequest keeps it (qualification.ts:108-111), the prompt enumerates pack.criteria (qualification.ts:140-141), CriteriaReport renders result.criteria straight from the pack with no hardcoded labels (CriteriaReport.tsx:119-153), and the qualify route persists req.classification onto the case (route.ts:98). On the case page the DB classification ('O-1B') flows into the criteria header ('O-1B criteria', CaseDetailView.tsx:143), EvidenceVault (CaseDetailView.tsx:196 -> buckets = criteriaNames(classification), EvidenceVault.tsx:55), and DraftStudio (CaseDetailView.tsx:204 -> draft prompt uses gate.value.classification, draftOperation.ts:151). The /visa/o-1b/artist landing reads packFor('O-1B') and pre-selects O-1B in the embedded screener (page.tsx:76,186). The share token encodes/decodes against the O-1B pack and rejects a status-count mismatch (letters-patent.ts:93-97). The O-1A-fallback trap that bites unknown classifications cannot bite her.
suggested_acceptance: Keep this wiring intact: O-1B must stay a live program and every classification-bearing surface must read packFor(classification), never a hardcoded O-1A list.
l2_priority: Run her O-1B profile live through /qualify and assert the rendered criteria are exactly the six arts names and that 'Scholarly articles'/'Judging'/'patent' never appear on screen.

title: Best-path finder scores her with a keyword mock that misreads a director — 'Lead role' and 'commercial success' miss on her real words
expected: The 'Find my best path' comparison should read a documentary director's record correctly: 'directed/director' as Lead role in distinguished productions, 'theatrical/streaming distribution deal' as commercial/critical success, and recommend O-1B with reasoning, not a coin-flip.
got: BestPathFinder calls /api/qualify/preview/best-path, which runs ONLY mockQualification keyword regex over every pack (best-path route:57-60, best-path.ts:71-98). Against the O-1B pack her likely words MISS on two of her strongest criteria: 'Lead role in distinguished productions' matches /lead role|starring|principal|headlin|featured performer/ — 'director'/'directed' is NOT in it (packs.ts:106); 'Record of major commercial or critical success' matches /box office|chart|sales|streams|.../ — 'distribution deal' and 'streaming' are NOT 'streams' (packs.ts:124). Worse, on the O-1A pack 'director' DOES match Critical role (packs.ts:79), so the keyword view literally treats her as a business critical-role — the exact misread she fears. She still tends to clear O-1B (~3/6 via recognition+reviews+org/expert) and O-1B outranks O-1A (2/8), so the recommendation usually lands right, but it lands right by luck of which keywords hit, the 'why' is keyword-thin, and her lead-creative role shows as unmet on the comparison card. Mitigated: she can skip best-path entirely via 'I already know my visa -> O-1B' (QualifyEntry.tsx:48-52), and the real model read on authenticated /qualify is the accurate one.
suggested_acceptance: Best-path is explicitly the keyless deterministic preview, so the fix is either (a) widen the O-1B 'Lead role' regex to include director/directed/helmed and 'commercial success' to include distribution/theatrical/streaming, or (b) clearly frame the comparison as a rough keyword pre-read so a director isn't told her lead role is 'not met'. L2 to judge whether a director sees O-1B recommended and isn't steered to O-1A.
l2_priority: Run her real documentary profile through 'Find my best path' live and assert O-1B is recommended and that the O-1A card does NOT outrank it by catching 'director' as Critical role.

title: Landing '/' certificate is the keyword mock — generic 'what we found' strings, not her named festivals/reviews
expected: A verdict that names HER evidence — Sundance/IDFA selections, the NYT Critic's Pick, the Variety review, the distribution deal, DGA/IDA membership — mapped to the right O-1B criterion.
got: The landing InstantVerdict posts to /api/qualify/preview which runs ONLY mockQualification (regex keyword match, Met/None only, templated generic evidence; route:69, qualification.ts:225-248). It shows the pack's canned line e.g. 'Mentions reviews or press coverage.' (packs.ts:119), not 'New York Times Critic's Pick'. The SAME O-1B six are shown (so no pack error) and the SoftGate honestly labels it 'an instant keyword read' that 'reads your whole record in depth' on the real screening (InstantVerdict.tsx:273-277), so it is disclosed and by-design, not deceptive. Her acceptance-criterion 2 (names her real evidence) is only satisfied on the authenticated /qualify model path.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the honest 'instant keyword read' label; ensure the soft-gate copy makes clear the named-evidence verdict comes from the deeper screening. No change required beyond the existing disclosure.
l2_priority: On authenticated /qualify with LLM_ENGINE=claude, assert the rendered O-1B evidence cells name HER festivals/reviews/deal, not the templated pack strings.

title: DraftStudio's user-facing copy hardcodes 'O-1A petition letter' even for her O-1B case
expected: An O-1B arts petitioner sees the draft tool described as drafting HER classification's letter (or neutrally), reinforcing she's on the right pack.
got: The actual draft prompt is classification-aware ('a U.S. ${req.classification} immigration petition letter', drafting.ts:177) and her sections are her qualifying O-1B criteria by name, so the OUTPUT is correct. But the visible UI string reads 'Draft a full O-1A petition letter from your scored criteria' (DraftStudio.tsx:376) and the module/critique header comments say 'O-1A' (drafting.ts:5,350,374 — stale comments). For a beneficiary whose single biggest fear is being handed an O-1A-shaped process, seeing 'O-1A' on the draft button copy is exactly the wrong word, even though the engine is right. A clarity/trust nick, not a pack error.
suggested_acceptance: Interpolate the classification into the DraftStudio intro copy (e.g. `Draft a full ${classification} petition letter...`) so an O-1B user never reads 'O-1A'; refresh the stale O-1A comments.
l2_priority: Confirm the draft button copy reads 'O-1B' (or neutral) on her case and the generated section headings are the arts six, not the O-1A eight.

title: Citation discipline + exhibit audit makes a fabricated festival/review/box-office number unable to ship unflagged
expected: The draft cites ONLY facts she supplied — zero fabricated festivals, review quotes, distributors, or box-office/streaming numbers.
got: buildDraftPrompt forbids inventing 'awards, publications, employers, dates, citation counts, or any other specifics' and fences applicant data as data-not-instructions (drafting.ts:181-192). On the DB path the draft is grounded on the persisted O-1B criteria + the vault's real exhibit facts (draftOperation.ts:147-161), and auditCitations quarantines any (Exhibit N) that doesn't resolve to a real on-file document (drafting.ts:593-608), surfaced in the ExhibitIndex (DraftStudio.tsx:447-454). The live adjudication gate also scans output for fabricated specifics / leaked visa code (draftOperation.ts:213-229). A fabricated Sundance year or a made-up box-office gross has no grounding path and an invented exhibit citation is flagged.
suggested_acceptance: Protect the no-fabrication rule + the (Exhibit N) resolution audit; don't relax them.
l2_priority: Feed a profile with NO box-office number and assert the live draft never invents one, and that an (Exhibit N) only appears when a matching vault doc exists.

title: Draft prompt has no arts-specific guidance — 'distinction' for a director isn't argued the way an arts O-1 petition does
expected: An arts-specialist associate argues a director's case as lead CREATIVE role in distinguished productions, critical reviews as the field's measure of acclaim, and festival selection as recognition — the arts grammar, not a business 'critical role' framing.
got: buildDraftPrompt is classification-generic: the only classification-specific lever is the criterion NAMES used as section headings (drafting.ts:174-208). There is no instruction that, for O-1B, 'lead role' means lead creative role in a distinguished production, that reviews are the field's recognition currency, or that a distribution deal evidences commercial/critical success. Whether the section prose reads like a real arts petition (vs a relabeled business argument) depends entirely on the live model's defaults from the criterion name + persisted evidence — invisible to static analysis. The persisted per-criterion evidence/rationale (not the raw CV) is the draft's grounding (accepted PN-DRAFT-01 / BACKLOG G1.3), viewed here through an arts lens.
suggested_acceptance: Live O-1B draft argues 'Lead role in distinguished productions' as lead CREATIVE role (director) in named distinguished productions and frames reviews/festival selection as the field's recognition — not a generic business 'critical role'.
l2_priority: Generate her full O-1B draft live and read the 'Lead role' + 'Reviews & press' sections: do they argue a director's distinction in arts terms, naming her festivals/reviews, or flatten to generic filler?

title: Evidence Vault buckets follow the O-1B pack; exhibit numbers are monotonic and never reused
expected: Each doc lands in the correct O-1B bucket (or honestly Unsorted), exhibit numbers are monotonic, the gap read reflects her real evidence, nothing invented.
got: EvidenceVault buckets = criteriaNames(classification) and summarizeVault(documents, classification) (EvidenceVault.tsx:55-56), fed the case's persisted O-1B classification (CaseDetailView.tsx:196). The categorize request sends that classification (EvidenceVault.tsx:72) so buildCategorizePrompt enumerates the O-1B six and coerces any off-list answer to 'Unsorted' (evidence.ts:106-148). Exhibit ordinals are assigned monotonically as a high-water mark across doc_seq and surviving rows, so deleting a doc never renumbers survivors (pglite-store.ts:646-693). summarizeVault only counts pack criteria toward coverage; an Unsorted/unknown bucket never closes a gap (evidence.ts:226-244). The UI is honest that 'documents present' != 'criterion proven' and that refiling doesn't re-check fit (EvidenceVault.tsx:210-216).
suggested_acceptance: Keep the classification-threaded buckets and the never-reused ordinal; don't let categorize default to O-1A when a caseId is present.
l2_priority: Add her NYT review, Sundance selection, distribution memo and DGA card; assert review lands under 'Reviews & press' (not Judging), the selection under recognition, the deal under commercial/critical success, and exhibit numbers stay monotonic across a delete.

title: Keyless categorize mock could mis-bucket her NYT review under recognition before 'Reviews & press' (order-dependent first-match)
expected: Her Variety/NYT review files under 'Reviews & press', not under 'National or international recognition' or 'Judging'.
got: The mock categorizer (used only when no engine is configured / on a reclaim) returns the FIRST pack criterion whose regex matches the name+content (evidence.ts:185-191). 'National or international recognition' (criterion 2) matches /review|.../? no, but it matches /acclaim|recogni[sz]ed|.../; a review doc that also says 'acclaimed' could hit criterion 2 before 'Reviews & press' (criterion 3) since iteration is in pack order. Under LLM_ENGINE=claude the real model classifies (route uses tryParseCategorizeResponse first, evidence.ts:84), so this only bites the keyless build; her pet peeve ('Variety review mis-bucketed') is a mock-only risk, not the live path. There is no O-1B 'Judging' bucket at all (good), so 'review under Judging' is structurally impossible for her.
suggested_acceptance: Live model path is the supported one and should bucket a review correctly; if the keyless mock matters, order 'Reviews & press' regex precedence or tighten the recognition regex so an explicit review doc isn't swallowed by 'recognition'.
l2_priority: On the live model path, paste a clearly-labelled NYT/Variety review and assert it lands under 'Reviews & press'.

title: Share card is DB-free, leaks no profile, renders the O-1B six, and is framed informational
expected: A credible arts verdict she'd send her producer; no private data in the token; not overclaiming; renders from the token alone.
got: LettersPatentShare encodes only name/classification/likelihood/per-criterion statuses (in pack order) into a base64url token, never the profile text (letters-patent.ts:69-77, LettersPatentShare.tsx:26-32). /c/[token] and the OG image decode it and render from packFor('O-1B') — six O-1B criterion badges — and decodeSnapshot rejects a tampered token whose status count != 6 (letters-patent.ts:93-97), so the coat-of-arms can't drift from the real pack. The card is labelled 'Certificate of Extraordinary Ability', a Stamp reads 'Qualifies'/'In progress' vs threshold, and the footer says 'Informational only - not legal advice' (page.tsx:118-120). Framing is celebratory but disclosed, not a legal grant.
suggested_acceptance: Keep the token profile-free and the pack-length integrity check; keep the informational footer.
l2_priority: Mint her positive O-1B verdict, open /c/[token] in a fresh session (no DB) and assert it shows the six arts criteria + her name + likelihood, and that the OG image renders 'x/6'.

title: Share affordance offers a link even for a below-threshold result — could let a thin verdict read as a 'certificate'
expected: The 'Letters Patent' certificate reads as a credible qualify verdict; a clearly-not-qualifying screen shouldn't be minted as a triumphant certificate she'd send a producer.
got: LettersPatentShare renders unconditionally on the result panel (QualifyPanel.tsx:271, InstantVerdict.tsx:201) regardless of whether the screening meets threshold; only the /c page Stamp downgrades to 'In progress' when qualifying < threshold (page.tsx:126). For Lucia (a strong, clearly-qualifying record) this is a non-issue and the honest 'In progress' stamp keeps it from overclaiming, so it's polish — noted because the share journey's DoD is 'a credible verdict, not overclaiming'.
suggested_acceptance: Optionally gate the share affordance (or soften its 'certificate' framing) when the result is below threshold; the existing 'In progress' stamp already mitigates overclaiming.
l2_priority: L2 to judge whether the share card credibly reads as an arts verdict a producer would respect (subjective, in-character).
